name: Vietnamese Pho
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Rice noodles 1/4-inch wide (or banh pho)
1 bn Fresh Asian or regular basil
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak bones overnight in cold water.
2. Place bones, oxtails, and flank steak in a large stock pot. Add water to cover and bring to a boil. Cook 10 minutes, then drain and rinse the pot and bones. Return bones to the pot, add 6 quarts of water, and bring to a boil. Skim scum and fat from the surface.
3. Stir bones at the bottom occasionally. Add 3 more quarts of water, bring to a boil again, and skim scum.
4. Lower heat and let simmer. Char clove-studded onions, shallots, and ginger under a broiler until they release their fragrant odors. Tie charred vegetables, star anise, and cinnamon stick in a thick, dampened cheesecloth.
5. Place the cheesecloth bundle in the stock with parsnips and salt. Simmer for 1 hour. Remove flank steak and continue simmering the broth uncovered for 4-5 hours. Add more water if needed to keep bones above the surface.
6. Slice beef sirloin against grain into paper-thin slices, about 2-by-2 inches. Slice flank steak the same way. Set aside.
7. Combine scallions and cilantro in a small bowl with half the sliced onions. Mix remaining onions with hot chili sauce. Soak rice noodles in warm water for 30 minutes. Drain and set aside.
8. When broth is ready, discard bones. Strain broth through a colander lined with a double layer of damp cheesecloth into a clean pot. Add fish sauce and bring to a boil. Reduce heat to simmer.
9. In another pot, bring 4 quarts of water to a boil. Add noodles and drain immediately. Do not overcook noodles.
10. Divide noodles among 4 large soup bowls. Top with sliced meats. Bring broth to a rolling boil, then ladle into soup bowls. Garnish with scallions mixture and black pepper.
11. Serve onions in hot chili sauce and remaining ingredients on the side as desired. You can also add Hoisin sauce as a dip. Serves 4.
